title: [BUG] Excel can not open the generated excel file with image
author: lijiming

comments.author: zurmokeeper
comments.body: @lijiming Can you provide the complete code? Post the base64 of the image or upload it?

comments.author: zurmokeeper
comments.body:
@lijiming 

A few questions:
1: You seem to have some problems with this base64 string, I found the online tool is not restored back to the original picture, you can try here (http://www.mf2.cn/img2base64/)
2: You can post the corresponding image of this base64 string

here is the image file. i open the excel file with WPS and saved the image. The origin base64 data is as below:

data:image/png;base64,

comments.author: lijiming
comments.body:
i checked the previous copied and pasted base64 data in code is wrong. The latest base64 data still can't be display in the website (http://www.mf2.cn/img2base64/), however, it can be displayed in my iPhone page, as shown below.
![2](https://github.com/exceljs/exceljs/assets/3368141/96f9055e-2a02-474a-b2a0-030d88659a7d)

comments.author: lijiming
comments.body: This is strange. The base64 data i got from console.log has corrupted?

comments.author: lijiming
comments.body: This is good, checked with the website  (http://www.mf2.cn/img2base64/).

comments.author: zurmokeeper
comments.body:
@lijiming 

`const base64Image = "data:image/png;base64,/9j/..........................................."
`

Write it like this and it will work. My local excel export already has that image. The value of base64 is not correct, and the part "data:image/png;base64," is missing in front of it, so add it.

comments.author: lijiming
comments.body: i have tried this with adding "data:image/png;base64,", it still doesn't work. I use workbook.xlsx.writeBuffer() write to buffer, covert to blob and then convert to base64 and then save to the base64 excel to file. This is strange, don't know where caused the issue.

comments.author: zurmokeeper
lijiming
comments.body:
Is this the base64 string you get here? Not the binary of an excel file? => yes, it is. the excel file can be open by excel w/o image. however, excel can opened it after addImage.

 And is uniapp only provides the API to save to file after the base64 conversion?  
 Is there no API to write binary content directly to a file? 
=> UniApp cannot save the file in the webview session (renderjs), doesn't provide file IO api.  

On the other side, i can't use Exceljs read/write IO function in UniApp iOS native environment. 
As a workaround, i pass the data from UniApp native environment to rednerjs webview through :prop, export to Excel using Exceljs, then writeBuffer -> BLOB -> readAsDataURL as base64 data, then pass the base64 data from webview to native environement and write the base64 data into Excel file in native environment.

it is complicated. It works if i don't addImage. I encountered issues after addImage for Excel opening, certain Excel version can fixed it after open (i tried Mac Excel), however, Excel 2021 on windows can't fix the file. WPS can open the file w/o issue.

comments.author: zurmokeeper
lijiming
zurmokeeper
comments.body: @lijiming Sorry, I'm really busy these days, maybe you can try asking a question here in [SF](https://segmentfault.com/questions), I'm still more used to Chinese Q&A.
